steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The blank joins two independent clauses: 'It began to dim over the next few weeks' and 'although they are extremely powerful, supernovas are short-lived phenomena,' with 'however' marking the contrast. Two valid placements exist for the conjunctive adverb: ending IC1 as a trailing parenthetical (', however;') or starting IC2 ('; however,'). Test which placement the sentence prefers.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — weeks, however;: The comma binds 'however' to IC1 as a trailing parenthetical, qualifying the dimming observation. The semicolon then joins the contrasting clause about supernovas being short-lived. Parse: [IC1 + trailing 'however']; [although DC, IC2]. This avoids the double-contrast problem and places the contrast exactly where the sentence intends it. ✓
  • Choice B — weeks. However,: Splits one continuous contrast into two sentences, misrepresenting the passage's unified structure. ✗
  • Choice C — weeks, however,: Comma before and comma after 'however' joins two independent clauses with only commas — a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice D — weeks; however,: The '; however,' pattern would open IC2 with 'however, although they are extremely powerful...' — stacking 'however' and 'although' back-to-back creates redundant double-contrast that is grammatically ungainly. ✗
A is correct.
